faqs.a: It usually covers the recommended treatment, the main risks and benefits, the likely alternatives including doing nothing, and a chance to ask questions. It applies to medications, therapy approaches, and procedures alike.

faqs.q: Is informed consent just signing a form?
faqs.a: No. The form documents the conversation, but the real consent is the understanding and agreement behind it. A clinician is meant to explain the plan in language you can follow, not just medical terms.

faqs.q: Can I change my mind after giving consent?
faqs.a: Yes. Consent is ongoing, so you can ask more questions later, revisit a choice, or withdraw consent as things unfold. Agreeing today doesn't lock you in forever.
